content.text: How can I recursively traverse a nested Python data structure like dictionaries and lists while creating a node for every element?
What happens if one of the nested objects refers back to an ancestor in the hierarchy (e.g., a self-referential list)?
Currently, this function will enter infinite recursion and cause a RecursionError because it does not track visited objects or cycles within the graph.
That makes sense; I'll need to add cycle detection if my data might contain loops.
